title: olearia stricta subsp. parvilobata
intro: Olearia stricta subsp. parvilobata, also known as the compact daisy bush, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Australia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The plant's leaves, stems, and flowers have been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, offering health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea, used in topical creams or ointments, or incorporated into capsules or tablets, making it a versatile remedy in traditional Australian medicine.
